page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The architecture allocates 1.0 thread per core, backed by 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Passmark memory scores and database operations, alongside top-tier multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier read speeds of 429.61 GB/sec and write speeds of 209.89 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

While multi-threaded workloads and memory-intensive operations perform exceptionally well, certain tasks show performance tradeoffs. For instance, LLM text generation using small models is relatively weak, though text generation for medium and large models remains strong. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, large-scale software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving. The ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise applications compared to traditional x86 architectures.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core performance, though single-core metrics remain average. Tradeoffs include weaker text generation speeds for smaller language models, contrasted by strong performance on larger models. This server is suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and high-speed network connectivity for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and web serving
